finding: Plan RL-005 revision 3 describes a different capability ontology than the code implements. Capability IDs in plan (secrets.read, session.hooks, tool.modify) don't match code's actual IDs (secrets.access, hooks.transform_message, tool.execute). The plan's Phase 2 pseudocode would not compile. Convergence from plan-to-code is broken — the plan doesn't describe the current state.

Appeal subagent proved Phase 1.5 is NOT independently reversible. The C→B→A chain is strict: Phase 2 trigger function at line 442 requires Phase 1.5's registrations array for pluginId lookup. Without registrations, all gated hooks fail with pluginId='unknown'. Plan's claim of 'independent reversible step' is falsified.

Coroner subagent scored debuggability at 2/10. Six failure scenarios average 46 min diagnosis. Worst case (bus subscriber staleness): 70 min. Three of six failures produce ZERO log output. Critical missing observability: silent hook deny at index.ts:438, no TOCTOU snapshot in trigger, bus subscriber captures stale closure, stripSecrets shallow-only with no WARN logs, no load-time fallback notification. The plan specifies 5 safety mechanisms that were never implemented.

Reasonable-doubt subagent scored testability at 7/10. Five capability scenarios testable in ≤10 lines. Three untestable (TOCTOU, bus subscriber, tool namespace pre-extraction) are acceptable — inherent integration concerns. Two blockers: stripSecrets() is private (not exported), sanitizePluginId() is inline at registry.ts:422 with no standalone export. Both need extraction per plan for testability. Existing test patterns (lightweight unit + heavy integration) support the proposed changes.

Exhibit-a overall error clarity: 5/10. 12 error paths audited. CRITICAL gaps: (1) Event hooks at lines 369-379 have ZERO error isolation — raw fn() calls without Effect.tryPromise; a single throwing handler crashes the event bus fiber. (2) stripSecrets SECRET_PATTERNS includes bare 'key' causing over-redaction of 'publicKey', 'keyboard', 'accessKeyId' etc — AND is flat-only so nested secrets (providers.*.apiKey) pass through. (3) Event denials produce zero log output — completely silent. HIGH gaps: no Effect.fn on trigger, TOCTOU live reference, no denial counters. 12 concrete recommendations included.
